mnemonics: C.H.A.N.G.E. (Cost of change Historically Almost Negligible, Grows Exponentially in traditional models)
A.G.I.L.E. (Adapts Gracefully to Iterative Learning & Evolution)
Remember: Waterfall is like concrete - harder to change as it sets. Agile is like clay - malleable throughout.
keyPoints: An agile process is iterative and incremental, focusing on rapid delivery and adaptability to changing requirements.
The core hypothesis of agile development is that the cost of change does not rise exponentially over time, and can be flattened with a iterative approach.
Agile processes emphasize customer collaboration, responding to change, and working software over comprehensive documentation and strict plans.
commonMistakes: Equating 'agile' with a complete lack of planning or documentation; it values them but prioritizes working software.
Assuming the cost of change is always low in agile; it requires good practices like continuous integration and refactoring to keep the cost flat.
Believing that any iterative process is inherently agile; agility specifically requires adaptability and customer collaboration.
